transcript_text: Hi, I recently invested in an equity mutual fund through my SIP. I want to know, if I redeem my units after 14 months, how will my gains be taxed? And will any TDS be deducted?

answer.query: Hi, I recently invested in an equity mutual fund through my SIP. I want to know, if I redeem my units after 14 months, how will my gains be taxed? And will any TDS be deducted?
answer.answer:
Based on your query, I'll address each part explicitly.

If you redeem your units after 14 months, your gains will be taxed as Long-Term Capital Gains (LTCG) since you're holding the units for more than 12 months. As per current tax laws (FY 2025-26), LTCG is taxed at 12.5% on gains exceeding Rs. 1.25 lakh in a financial year.

Regarding TDS, it will not be deducted from your mutual fund redemption as you are a resident Indian investor.

Sources: Q9, Q10

retrieved_chunks.text:
FAQ Q9: What is the tax treatment on equity mutual fund gains?
Section: Taxation
Answer: As per current tax laws (FY 2025-26): Short-Term Capital Gains (STCG) — for units held less than 12 months — are taxed at 20%. Long-Term Capital Gains (LTCG) — for units held 12 months or more — are taxed at 12.5% on gains exceeding Rs. 1.25 lakh in a financial year. No indexation benefit is available for equity funds. Note: Tax laws are subject to change. Please consult your tax advisor for personalised advice.

metadata.question: What is the tax treatment on equity mutual fund gains?

retrieved_chunks.text:
FAQ Q10: Will TDS be deducted on mutual fund redemptions?
Section: Taxation
Answer: TDS is not applicable for resident Indian investors on mutual fund redemptions. For NRI investors, TDS is deducted at source at applicable rates — 30% on STCG for equity funds and 20% on LTCG. Form 15G/15H can be submitted by eligible residents for dividend payouts only.

metadata.question: Will TDS be deducted on mutual fund redemptions?

retrieved_chunks.text:
FAQ Q8: Can I redeem only a part of my investment?
Section: Redemption & Payouts
Answer: Yes, partial redemption is allowed. You can redeem either a specific number of units or a specific rupee amount. The minimum redemption amount is Rs. 500 or 50 units, whichever is lower, subject to the scheme's exit load policy.

metadata.question: Can I redeem only a part of my investment?
